title: Smoky Shredded Mexican Pork Cochinita Pibil
description: A classic Mexican dish from the Yucatan region, featuring smoky shredded pork in a citrusy marinade with hints of spices and herbs.
instructions: Combine orange and lime juice, cumin, oregano, salt, and black pepper in a blender or food processor to make the marinade.
Add chopped onions and garlic to the marinade and blend until smooth.
Rub the marinade all over the pork shoulder, making sure it's fully coated.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Place the pork shoulder in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot.
Roast in the preheated oven for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
Shred the pork with two forks and stir in chopped cilantro leaves.
Serve the shredded pork with warm tortillas, rice, and your favorite toppings.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
